text.data-narration: A search engine architecture refers to the overall design and organization of a search engine's infrastructure, including its components, data structures, and algorithms.

g.text: A search engine architecture refers to the overall design and organization of a search engine's infrastructure, including its components, data structures, and algorithms.

text.data-narration: The process of building a massive database of web pages, where each page is represented by a unique identifier and a set of metadata (e.g., title, keywords, content).

g.text: The process of building a massive database of web pages, where each page is represented by a unique identifier and a set of metadata (e.g., title, keywords, content).

text.data-narration: The system's ability to parse and interpret user queries, and return relevant results.

g.text: The system's ability to parse and interpret user queries, and return relevant results.

text.data-narration: The process of assigning a score to each relevant page, based on its relevance to the query.

g.text: The process of assigning a score to each relevant page, based on its relevance to the query.

text.data-narration: The process of retrieving the top-ranked pages from the index.

g.text: The process of retrieving the top-ranked pages from the index.

text.data-narration: A link analysis algorithm developed by Google, which assigns a score to each page based on the number and quality of links pointing to it.

g.text: A link analysis algorithm developed by Google, which assigns a score to each page based on the number and quality of links pointing to it.
